# Feature: Documentation refresh

## 1. Feature Goal

**ID**: FEAT-007
**Source Plan**: `.spec-lite/plan.md`

Bring the Read the Docs documentation in line with the refactored library: rewrite
the rst pages for the unified API, correct the false "works with any event loop"
claim, document the return-type duality, cancellation, process-pool/pickle
constraints, and resource lifecycle, add a migration guide from the old API, and
wire Sphinx autodoc/napoleon so the API reference tracks docstrings. No emojis, rst
format, hosted on readthedocs.io as today.

Business value: the API is the product here, and the docs are how users meet it.
The current docs describe the old (removed) surface and make at least one
incorrect claim; accurate, clear docs are part of "solidifying" the library.

## 2. Data Model (Granular)

Not applicable (documentation only).

## 3. Files

- `docs/source/index.rst` — landing page; updated toctree (add caveats + migration).
- `docs/source/features.rst` — corrected feature list (fix the event-loop claim; no emojis).
- `docs/source/basicusage.rst` — rewritten for the unified `asyncify`, properties.
- `docs/source/api.rst` — rewritten: `executor=` param, mixins, config/lifecycle, removed surface.
- `docs/source/caveats.rst` — **new**: cancellation, process-pool/pickle constraints, resource lifecycle/shutdown, per-item iterator cost, asyncio-only loop support.
- `docs/source/migration.rst` — **new**: old API -> new API mapping.
- `docs/source/conf.py` — enable `sphinx.ext.autodoc` + `sphinx.ext.napoleon`; ensure the package is importable for autodoc; pin theme; set version.
- `docs/source/apireference.rst` — **new** (optional): autodoc-generated reference from docstrings.
- `README.md` — quick example updated to the new API; install/import names; CI badge (from FEAT-005).
- `tests/samples.py` -> migrate runnable examples into docs or a `docs/examples/` dir; remove `print`-heavy sample from the package test path.

## 4. Dependencies

- **FEAT-001..004** (required): docs describe the implemented public API; write/finalize after the code features land (small drift is fine to fix iteratively).
- **FEAT-005** (related): docs optional-dependencies and the autodoc build; CI badge.

## 5. Implementation Tasks

### TASK-001: Rewrite `features.rst` and correct the event-loop claim

- [ ] **Implementation**: Update the feature bullets to the unified API. **Correct** the "compatible with any other event loops (uvloop, trio, ...)" claim: `unblock` relies on `loop.run_in_executor`, so it works with **asyncio-compatible** loops (asyncio, uvloop) but **not** trio/curio (which are not asyncio). State this plainly. Remove the "Python 3.7 and beyond" item; state 3.10+. Remove any emojis.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: N/A (docs). Validation: `sphinx-build -W` (warnings as errors) succeeds.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: (this task is the doc update).
- **Verify**: `sphinx-build -b html docs/source docs/_build -W` builds; the trio claim is gone/corrected.

### TASK-002: Rewrite `basicusage.rst` for the unified API

- [ ] **Implementation**: Update examples to `@asyncify`, `@asyncify(executor="process")`, the class decorator (`@asyncify`, `@asyncify(include=/exclude=)`), `async_property`, `async_cached_property`. Remove the old `asyncify_pp = asyncify_pp(func)` non-decorator workaround (the process decorator now works). Keep examples copy-pasteable and runnable (`asyncio.run(...)`).
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: N/A. Optional: doctest or a CI step that executes the example snippets.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: (this task is the doc update).
- **Verify**: Snippets run under Python 3.10+; build passes.

### TASK-003: Rewrite `api.rst` (executor param, mixins, config, removed surface)

- [ ] **Implementation**: Document the unified `asyncify` signature; the four mixins (`AsyncMixin`, `AsyncIterMixin`, `AsyncContextMixin`, `AsyncContextIterMixin`) with the `executor=` class keyword and the wrapper pattern; the consolidated close/`aclose`/`call_close_on_exit` rule; config/lifecycle (`set_thread_pool`, `set_process_pool`, `shutdown`). Replace the old `_unblock_methods_to_asynchify` examples entirely.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: N/A.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: (this task is the doc update).
- **Verify**: Build passes; no references to removed symbols remain (`grep` for `asyncify_pp`, `AsyncBase`, `_unblock_methods_to_asynchify`, `set_event_loop` returns nothing in `docs/`).

### TASK-004: New `caveats.rst`

- [ ] **Implementation**: Document: the return-type duality contract (Future when a loop runs vs coroutine otherwise); cancellation limits (thread/process work already started cannot be force-killed); process-pool constraints (module-level callables only; closures/lambdas rejected; args/results must be picklable; the trampoline mechanism at a high level); resource lifecycle (default pools, `shutdown()`, `atexit`, custom executors); per-item cost of async iteration; asyncio-only loop support. Keep the (corrected) pickle note as a usability constraint, not a security claim.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: N/A.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: (this task is the doc update).
- **Verify**: Build passes; caveats page in the toctree.

### TASK-005: New `migration.rst` (old -> new)

- [ ] **Implementation**: Provide a mapping table: `asyncify_pp` -> `asyncify(executor="process")`; `asyncify_func`/`asyncify_func_pp`/`asyncify_cls` -> `asyncify`; `AsyncBase`/`AsyncPPBase` + `_unblock_methods_to_asynchify` -> `@asyncify(include=/exclude=)` or `AsyncMixin(executor=...)`; the 8 iter/ctx base classes -> the four mixins / protocol auto-detection; `set_event_loop` -> removed (running loop is always used); `Registry` -> removed (internal); `UnblockException` -> `UnblockError`. Include before/after snippets for the common cases.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: N/A.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: (this task is the doc update).
- **Verify**: Build passes; every removed symbol from plan §5 appears in the mapping.

### TASK-006: Sphinx autodoc/napoleon + conf.py + samples migration

- [ ] **Implementation**: Enable `sphinx.ext.autodoc` and `sphinx.ext.napoleon` in `conf.py`; ensure `unblock` is importable during the build (RTD config / path). Add `apireference.rst` using `automodule`/`autofunction` to render docstrings (the source of truth per the coding standards). Update `version`/`release` and the `.readthedocs.yaml` if needed. Migrate the useful runnable examples out of `tests/samples.py` into the docs (or `docs/examples/`), and drop the stray `print`-driven sample from the package.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: N/A.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: (this task is the doc update).
- **Verify**: `sphinx-build -W` renders the autodoc reference with the actual public symbols; RTD build configuration validated.

### TASK-007: Update README + project URLs

- [ ] **Implementation**: Update the README quick-start to the new API; clarify `pip install get-unblock` (distribution) vs `import unblock`; add the CI badge (FEAT-005); refresh the release notes section to describe the refactor. Verify `pyproject.toml` `[project.urls]` still resolve.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: N/A.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: (this task is the doc update).
- **Verify**: README example runs on 3.10+; links resolve.

- **Error Handling**: Docs accurately describe when `UnblockError` is raised (misuse, invalid executor, unpicklable callable).
